persona: I’m looking for a GMC Crew Cab Pickup with an automatic transmission and four-wheel drive—something capable, roomy, and ready for real use. The most important thing for me is that it’s Large, and I also care a lot about keeping MSRP at most $43,210, so size and budget are my main anchors. I’m aiming for at least 23 highway MPG, and that matters quite a bit, though I could compromise slightly if the truck otherwise feels perfect. I’d like 2013 or newer, but I’m relatively flexible on year compared to the other priorities.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Cadillac Sedan with a AUTOMATIC transmission and rear wheel drive.
full_query_sentence: I am looking for a Cadillac Sedan with a AUTOMATIC transmission and rear wheel drive, is large size, has MSRP at most $58,010, runs on premium unleaded (required), gets at least 16 city MPG.
persona: I want a Cadillac sedan with an automatic transmission and rear-wheel drive that feels comfortable, confident, and properly premium. The biggest thing for me is getting at least 16 city MPG, because I do enough city driving that I don’t want something that guzzles fuel. I’m also trying to keep MSRP at most $58,010, and I care about staying under that, though I’m somewhat flexible if the car feels like a great deal. I’d like it to be Large, but I can compromise on exact size more easily than I can compromise on city mileage. Ideally it runs on premium unleaded (required), but I’m the most willing to bend on fuel requirement if everything else is excellent.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Volkswagen 2dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATED_MANUAL transmission and front wheel drive.
full_query_sentence: I am looking for a Volkswagen 2dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATED_MANUAL transmission and front wheel drive, is a Beetle, gets at least 31 highway MPG, runs on premium unleaded (recommended), has MSRP at most $26,550.
persona: I’m looking for a Volkswagen 2-door hatchback with an automated manual transmission and front-wheel drive—something fun, distinctive, and easy to park. The biggest thing for me is that it’s specifically a Beetle, and I also care a lot about getting at least 31 highway MPG and using premium unleaded (recommended), since I want it to feel efficient and a bit punchy. I’m aiming for MSRP at most $26,550, but I’m fairly flexible on price if it’s the exact Beetle setup I want. Overall, I’m more willing to compromise on budget than on the model and the general driving vibe.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Chevrolet Sedan with a MANUAL transmission and front wheel drive.
full_query_sentence: I am looking for a Chevrolet Sedan with a MANUAL transmission and front wheel drive, is from 2009 or newer, is compact size, gets at least 28 city MPG, has MSRP at most $16,620.
persona: I want a Chevrolet sedan with a manual transmission and front-wheel drive, something compact and straightforward with a little driver involvement. My top priority is that it’s 2009 or newer, because I don’t want to deal with something too old or outdated. I also care about keeping MSRP at most $16,620, and I’m fairly serious about that budget cap. I prefer it to be Compact, but I can compromise a bit on size if the car otherwise fits. I’m aiming for at least 28 city MPG, though I’m the most flexible on city mileage if the year and price are right.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Toyota 4dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.
full_query_sentence: I am looking for a Toyota 4dr Hatchback with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive, has MSRP at most $24,495, gets at least 36 highway MPG, gets at least 30 city MPG, is from 2017 or newer.
persona: I’m looking for a Toyota 4-door hatchback with an automatic transmission and front-wheel drive—mainly a reliable, efficient daily car. The biggest priorities for me are getting at least 30 city MPG and having it be 2017 or newer, because I really want modern efficiency and a newer feel. I’m also trying to keep MSRP at most $24,495, and I’m moderately flexible, but I’d rather not stretch too much. I’d like at least 36 highway MPG, but I’m more willing to compromise on highway mileage than on city MPG and model year.

base_query_sentence: I am looking for a Volvo Wagon with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive.
full_query_sentence: I am looking for a Volvo Wagon with a AUTOMATIC transmission and front wheel drive, has MSRP at most $32,850, runs on regular unleaded, is midsize size, is from 2008 or newer.
persona: I want a Volvo wagon with an automatic transmission and front-wheel drive, something practical with a calm, comfortable vibe. My biggest priority is it being 2008 or newer, since I want something that still feels reasonably modern and dependable. I also care a lot about it running on regular unleaded, because I prefer easier fuel costs and hassle-free ownership. I’d like it to be Midsize, and I’m moderately flexible there if it still feels roomy. For MSRP I’m aiming at most $32,850, but I’m the most willing to compromise on price if the year and fuel type fit perfectly.
